title: Run Argus-driven visual and behavioral overhaul pass for Atelier tools
operator_request: After native/state work, visually and behaviorally overhaul CKC/Posekit inside Handshake so it mirrors the original app more, with Argus checking panels, tabs, overview, navigation, layout, and style.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Only start after main surfaces exist. Use Argus headlessly; do not pop the app foreground for visual inspection.

scope.summary: Polish the live Atelier product experience against original CKC/Posekit workflow intent, using Argus as the proof path.
base_scope: Inspect every Atelier tab and major state through Argus snapshots/screenshots.
Fix clutter, overlapping text, confusing labels, dead controls, and incoherent navigation.
Check CKC book layout, Posekit split view, Ingest review flow, and manual discoverability.
Record remaining visual/behavioral gaps as follow-up MTs instead of claiming parity.
Update internal UserManual if navigation or behavior changes.
acceptance_criteria: Argus can see and steer all core Atelier workflows.
Layouts are readable and do not overlap at tested desktop/constrained sizes.
Residual gaps are explicit and not represented as complete.
verification: Focused UI tests for navigation and layout states.
Adversarial visual review before and after fixes.

manual_requirement: Required for any changed behavior or navigation.
